new_method.method:
Motivation
- Problem: HighвҖ‘dimensional (dвүҲ10^2вҖ“10^5) estimation of specific stochasticвҖ‘Taylor/LieвҖ‘bracket coefficients for E[g(X_T)] under x,ОёвҖ‘dependent diffusions is computationally prohibitive (dense O(d^2вҖ“d^3) derivative flows) and statistically brittle (variance explodes when isolating orderвҖ‘KвүӨ3 terms).
- Why now: NeuralвҖ‘SDE training, highвҖ‘dimensional Greeks with local/stochastic volatility, and weakвҖ‘Taylor PDE fixes increasingly require orderвҖ‘2/3 corrections and parameter sensitivities in regimes where diffusion ranks are lowвҖ‘toвҖ‘moderate (effective rвүӘd), and where compute must focus on the SDEвҖҷs actual occupancy rather than uniform grids.
- Gaps: (i) Avoid dense spatial derivative flows when Пғ(x,Оё) varies; (ii) directly target orderвҖ‘K coefficients (not just values/gradients); (iii) allocate evaluations to few, highвҖ‘leverage timeвҖ“state nodes; (iv) collapse variance with coefficientвҖ‘aware critics; (v) endвҖ‘toвҖ‘end bias/variance control with MLMC and principled time randomization; (vi) robustness to nonвҖ‘smooth drifts.

Methodology: HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ (HighвҖ‘dimensional LowвҖ‘rank Stochastic Taylor Probing)
Core principle: Estimate orderвҖ‘KвүӨ3 weakвҖ‘Taylor coefficients via Stratonovich LieвҖ‘bracket functionals using only JacobianвҖ‘vector products (JVPs), randomized bracket contractions, diffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction, and coefficientвҖ‘specific Poisson/BSDE control variates. Integrate an occupancyвҖ‘aware node selector (momentвҖ‘guided RPC) and MLMC coupling for biasвҖ“variance control.

1) Coefficient calculus and targeting
- HallвҖ‘basis expansion: Express target weakвҖ‘Taylor coefficients for E[g(X_T)] in a minimal Hall basis of Stratonovich brackets of b and {Пғ_j} (e.g., [Пғ_i,Пғ_j], [b,Пғ_i], [Пғ_i,[Пғ_j,Пғ_k]]). Provide an automatic ItГҙвҶ’Stratonovich converter via directional JVPs to evaluate b^вҲҳ = b вҲ’ 1/2 ОЈ_j (вҲӮПғ_j)Пғ_j without forming dense Jacobians.
- ObservableвҖ‘aware weights: A small library maps userвҖ‘supplied g to the required basis elements and their weights, returning both coefficient definitions and parameter sensitivities вҲӮ/вҲӮОё with the same JVP machinery.
- BracketвҖ‘sparsity screening (new): Use a few Hutchinson/Hadamard probes to detect negligible bracket contractions at candidate nodes; prune basis elements adaptively before heavy evaluation.

2) Directional LieвҖ‘bracket probing (DLP)
- JVPвҖ‘only recursion: Compute bracket actions via recursive JVP rules (Г  la LogвҖ‘NCDE), avoiding Jacobians/Hessians and reducing costs to batched JVPs.
- Randomized contractions: Estimate bracket contractions with Hutchinson/Hutch++/orthogonal Hadamard probes; share probe seeds across time and MLMC levels for strong coupling.
- DiffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction: Estimate local active diffusion subspace r via randomized range finding on empirical Gram of Пғ(x,t); restrict all probing to this rвҖ‘dimensional subspace to achieve O(mВ·r) per node.
- Probe alignment (new): Use a streaming Oja/Krylov update to align probes with top curvature directions of bracket contractions at selected nodes, cutting probe counts m to O(log r) under spectral decay.
- Krylov caching: Reuse small Krylov subspaces of differential operators along nearby nodes and across MLMC levels to amortize JVP costs.

3) CoefficientвҖ‘specific Poisson/BSDE control variates
- PerвҖ‘coefficient critics: For each coefficient with integrand h_c(t,x), train a critic c_ПҲ solving L c вүҲ h_c (or BSDE counterpart). Use S_ПҲ = вҲ« Пғ^TвҲҮc_ПҲ dW as a zeroвҖ‘mean control variate. Optimise ПҲ with detachвҖ‘style losses (no second derivatives) or a gradientвҖ‘network variant that approximates вҲҮc directly.
- Spectral preconditioning (new): Precondition critic training by projecting both L and h_c into the estimated diffusion subspace; apply a NeumannвҖ‘series preconditioner for illвҖ‘conditioned L.
- Shared trunk, perвҖ‘head critics (new): MultiвҖ‘task critic architecture shares a backbone across coefficients and adds lightweight heads, exploiting correlation among h_c to reduce sample/compute.
- Steinised warmвҖ‘start: Stabilise early training with a scoreвҖ‘based Stein loss under the moment prior (see 4), avoiding second derivatives and poor early estimates.

4) MomentвҖ‘guided RPC over joint timeвҖ“state
- OccupancyвҖ‘aware prior: Build a cheap Gaussian prior N(m(t),P(t)) for X_t via assumedвҖ‘density evolution (linearization or symmetric 3rdвҖ‘order cubature). Use it to whiten states for kernel definition.
- BracketвҖ‘aware multiвҖ‘task RPC (new): Run RPC in the product space (t,x) with whitening by P(t), and use a blockвҖ‘kernel that upweights locations with large proxy Var[h_c]; perform multiвҖ‘task RPC so one node set serves many coefficients with optimal weights from a small linear system. Increase nodes until an RPC residual tolerance is met.

5) Multilevel time randomization and drift tilting
- Time randomization: Convert path integrals to TВ·E[h(П„,X_П„)] with П„~Unif[0,T] to reduce variance and enable sharedвҖ‘seed MLMC coupling.
- MLMC coupling: Couple Brownian paths and probe seeds across levels; adaptively allocate samples per level from online variance/bias estimates.
- Learned small tilts: Apply lightweight Girsanov drift tilting toward highвҖ‘leverage regions guided by bracket magnitudes; correct via RadonвҖ“Nikodym weights.

6) Robustness to nonвҖ‘smooth drifts
- Light Gaussian mollification for piecewiseвҖ‘smooth drifts (e.g., ReLU nets). Track total MSE as mollification + discretization + probing + quadrature errors; apply firstвҖ‘order Stein deвҖ‘biasing under the moment prior when needed.

7) PlugвҖ‘ins and interfaces
- GeneratorвҖ‘gradient surrogate: Provide unbiased surrogates for вҲҮv and Hessians needed by generatorвҖ‘gradient estimators, replacing O(d^2вҖ“d^3) stateвҖ‘derivative flows with O(mВ·r) JVPs at a handful of RPC nodes.
- PDE solver hooks: Emit orderвҖ‘K weakвҖ‘Taylor correction coefficients at RPC nodes to boost fixedвҖ‘step Kolmogorov PDE solvers.
- Parameter sensitivities (new): Return вҲӮ/вҲӮОё of each Taylor coefficient via the same probing infrastructure, enabling highвҖ‘dimensional Greeks and stable neuralвҖ‘SDE training signals.

8) Theory and diagnostics
- Guarantees: Prove consistency of bracketвҖ‘coefficient estimators under JVPвҖ‘only randomized contractions; derive variance bounds under spectral decay of bracket contractions and diffusionвҖ‘subspace approximation error. Show zeroвҖ‘variance limit when c_ПҲвүҲu (Poisson solution), up to probing/discretization noise.
- MSE decomposition: MSE вүӨ C1В·(Hall trunc.)^2 + C2В·(О”t)^{2p} + C3В·RPC residual + C4В·Var(probe)/N + C5В·Var(CV misspec.) + C6В·mollification^2.
- Complexity: With r the active diffusion rank and m=O(log r) Hutch++ probes, perвҖ‘node cost is near O(mВ·r) and total cost O(mВ·rВ·KВ·n_nodes); node counts controlled by RPC residual.
- Online controllers: Jackknife for probeвҖ‘count m; RPC residual for node budget; perвҖ‘coefficient CV gap to tune critic capacity; MLMC level allocation via sampleвҖ‘variance estimates.

9) Implementation blueprint (PyTorch + functorch + torchsde)
- Bracket/JVP engine: HallвҖ‘basis combinators up to K=3 with batched vmap JVPs and Krylov caching.
- Probe generators: Rademacher/Gaussian/Hadamard; Hutch++ with onвҖ‘theвҖ‘fly orthogonalization; probeвҖ‘alignment via Oja updates.
- Critics: Small MLP/CNN with detach/gradientвҖ‘network variants; diffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning; Stein regularizer under moment prior; streaming training during estimation.
- RPC: GPUвҖ‘friendly, whitened by P(t); multiвҖ‘task blockвҖ‘kernel for coefficients; small linear solves with caching.
- MLMC: Coupled Brownian bridges and shared probe seeds; builtвҖ‘in time randomization; adaptive level/sample selection.
- APIs: (a) orderвҖ‘K Taylor coefficients and вҲӮ/вҲӮОё thereof, (b) generatorвҖ‘gradient plugвҖ‘ins, (c) weakвҖ‘Taylor PDE correction coefficients.

How this advances beyond prior work
- GeneratorвҖ‘gradient: Eliminates dense xвҖ‘derivative flows by JVPвҖ‘only bracket probing with diffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction; adds coefficientвҖ‘specific Poisson/BSDE critics and multiвҖ‘task RPC over timeвҖ“stateвҖ”none present in generatorвҖ‘gradient.
- BSDEвҖ‘based PDE solvers: ReвҖ‘targets the zeroвҖ‘variance principle to individual Taylor integrands, not to values/gradients; provides secondвҖ‘orderвҖ‘free training (detach/gradientвҖ‘network) specialized per coefficient with diffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning.
- LogвҖ‘NCDE: Repurposes their LieвҖ‘bracket JVP machinery for estimation, not modeling; adds occupancyвҖ‘aware RPC node selection, MLMC time randomization, and coefficientвҖ‘aware control variates.
- RPC/KQ/EZQ: First application to joint timeвҖ“state node selection under SDE occupancy with multiвҖ‘task, bracketвҖ‘aware kernels; integrates moment ODEs purely as whitening/proposal (correctness does not assume Gaussian marginals).
- AssumedвҖ‘density FPK: Uses moment ODEs strictly for proposals/whitening and Stein deвҖ‘biasing; does not replace path sampling nor assume Gaussian correctness.

Evaluation plan
- Benchmarks: (i) HighвҖ‘d Greeks with local/stochastic volatility and xвҖ‘dependent Пғ; (ii) neuralвҖ‘SDE training (gradient/Hessian stability, train time to target loss); (iii) Kolmogorov PDEs with fixed steps (weakвҖ‘Taylor correction vs baseline error); (iv) synthetic SDEs with known Taylor coefficients to assess accuracy/bias.
- Baselines: GeneratorвҖ‘gradient, pathwise differentiation, BSDE critics (value/gradient only), simple MC without coefficient targeting.
- Metrics: Runtime scaling vs d and effective rank r; probe/node counts for target error; perвҖ‘coefficient variance/bias; gradient variance in neuralвҖ‘SDE training; PDE error at equal timesteps.
- Ablations: JVPвҖ‘only vs explicit Jacobians; probe alignment; diffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction; multiвҖ‘task vs singleвҖ‘task RPC; critic variants; MLMC/time randomization; drift tilting.

Expected impact
- Computational: Feasible K=2вҖ“3 stochasticвҖ‘Taylor estimation in dвүҲ10^3вҖ“10^5 by turning O(d^2вҖ“d^3) flows into O(mВ·r) JVPs evaluated at a few occupancyвҖ‘selected nodes; further amortization via Krylov caching and MLMC.
- Statistical: CoefficientвҖ‘specific critics with diffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning approach zeroвҖ‘variance limits; MLMC/time randomization stabilize residual variance and discretization bias; adaptive controllers yield automatic toleranceвҖ‘driven computation.
- Applications:
  вҖў HighвҖ‘dimensional Greeks under stateвҖ‘dependent diffusions.
  вҖў LowвҖ‘variance gradients/Hessians for scalable neuralвҖ‘SDE training.
  вҖў WeakвҖ‘Taylor corrections for PDE solvers at fixed steps in high d.
  вҖў HigherвҖ‘order corrections to effective SGD dynamics near critical points without dense flows.
- Deliverables: OpenвҖ‘source PyTorch library with APIs for coefficient/gradient estimation and PDE plugвҖ‘ins; reproducible benchmarks demonstrating scaling, accuracy, and variance reductions.

experimental_design.experiment_strategy:
Title: Experimental plans for validating HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ (KвүӨ3 weakвҖ‘Taylor coefficient estimation via JVPвҖ‘only LieвҖ‘bracket probing, multiвҖ‘task critics, RPC, MLMC)

Experiment 1: Synthetic linear/nonlinear SDEs with groundвҖ‘truth or highвҖ‘precision references
Objective and hypotheses
- Validate correctness and variance gains of coefficient targeting, JVPвҖ‘only bracket probing, diffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction, probe alignment, and coefficientвҖ‘specific critics.
- H1: Estimated K=2/3 coefficients reconstruct E[g(X_T)] with O(T^{K+1}) residual and nearвҖ‘zero bias as О”tвҶ’0.
- H2: Runtime scales with d through r (active diffusion rank) and probes mвүҲO(log r), not dense O(d^2) flows.
- H3: Critics reduce variance by вүҘ10Г— on nontrivial coefficients; RPC cuts node count by вүҘ3Г— at fixed tolerance.

Models and observables
- Case A (commuting diffusions, sparsity check): dвҲҲ{256, 2k, 10k}, r=8. ItГҙ SDE: dX = b(X) dt + ОЈ_j Пғ_j(X) dW_j with Пғ_j(X)=e_j f_j(x_j) (componentвҖ‘wise functions), and b(x)=вҲ’Оӣx + c, Оӣ diagonal. Many brackets vanish: [Пғ_i,Пғ_j]=0 (iвү j). Observable g(x)=xбөҖQx with Q sparse banded.
- Case B (nonвҖ‘commuting, lowвҖ‘rank linear, analytic reference for small d): dвҲҲ{16, 64}, rвҲҲ{2,4}. Stratonovich linear fields: b(x)=A x, Пғ_k(x)=B_k x. Then [Пғ_i,Пғ_j](x)=(B_i B_jвҲ’B_j B_i) x, etc. Choose g(x)=xбөҖQx. Analytic E[x xбөҖ] via Lyapunov ODE; also analytic bracket integrands via matrix commutators allow closedвҖ‘form coefficients at small T.
- Case C (nonlinear, highвҖ‘d scaling with MC reference): dвҲҲ{512, 2k}, rвҲҲ{4,8}. b(x)=вҲ’Оӣx + О· tanh(Cx), Пғ(x)=U diag(s(x)) with UвҲҲR^{dГ—r}, s_i(x)=вҲҡ(a_i + b_i x_i^2). Observable g(x)=xбөҖQx and a cubic g(x)=вҲ‘ w_i x_i^3 to exercise K=3.

Ground truth/reference
- Case A: Many coefficients provably zero; nonzero ones validated via highвҖ‘precision MC (1e7 samples, О”t=2^{вҲ’12}).
- Case B: Use matrix ODEs for mean/covariance to get exact E[g(X_T)] and, for very small T, exact weakвҖ‘Taylor coefficients from commutator formulae. CrossвҖ‘check against our estimates.
- Case C: Use MLMC with L=6 levels and N_L tuned for reference MSE < 1eвҲ’4 as gold standard for E[g(X_T)].

Compared methods
- HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ full: JVPвҖ‘only DLP + diffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction + probe alignment (Oja) + Hutch++ + multiвҖ‘task RPC + coefficientвҖ‘specific critics + time randomization + MLMC coupling.
- Ablations: remove each component in turn (no RPC, no critics, no subspace restriction, no probe alignment, no MLMC/timeвҖ‘rand, singleвҖ‘task critics, no bracketвҖ‘sparsity screening, naГҜve Gaussian probes).
- DenseвҖ‘Jac baseline (small d only): Explicit Jacobians/Hessians to form brackets and coefficients (demonstrates scaling and numeric stability limits).

Metrics
- Accuracy: Absolute/relative error of each coefficient and of reconstructed E[g(X_T)] using K=2/3 expansion; logвҖ‘log slope vs T to verify O(T^{K+1}).
- Variance: Sample variance of coefficient estimators; controlвҖ‘variate reduction factor (CVR).
- Efficiency: WallвҖ‘time to ОөвҖ‘MSE; scaling vs d and r; number of JVPs; node count from RPC; probes m used; memory.
- Sparsity detection: Precision/recall for zero brackets in Case A after bracketвҖ‘sparsity screening.

Protocol
1) Implement JVPвҖ‘only bracket engine (PyTorch+functorch): Hall basis up to K=3; Stratonovich converter via JVP: b^вҲҳ(x)=b(x)вҲ’0.5 ОЈ_j J(Пғ_j)(x) Пғ_j(x) via directional JVPs.
2) DiffusionвҖ‘subspace estimation: At each candidate node, randomized range finder on empirical Gram of Пғ(x): draw qвҲҲR^r probes, Y=Пғ(x)Q, do one power iteration; retain rМӮ top cols; project probes.
3) Randomized contractions: Hutch++ with k=O(log rМӮ) Gaussian/Hadamard probes + Oja alignment updated per node cache.
4) RPC node selection in (t,x): Build moment prior N(m(t),P(t)) by assumedвҖ‘density evolution (linearization). Compute blockвҖ‘kernel weights from proxy Var[h_c]; grow nodes until residual < П„.
5) Critics: Shared trunk MLP, perвҖ‘coefficient heads; loss: detachвҖ‘style Poisson residual with diffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning; Stein warmвҖ‘start epochs.
6) MLMC + time randomization: Express integrals as TВ·E[h(П„,X_П„)] with П„~Unif; couple Brownian bridges and probe seeds across levels; online varianceвҖ‘based allocation.
7) Data sweeps: TвҲҲ{0.05,0.1,0.2}; d, r as above; m initialized at 8, adapted by jackknife.
8) For Case B smallвҖ‘T, also compute analytic coefficients for [Пғ_i,Пғ_j] and [b,Пғ_i] contributions and compare termвҖ‘wise.

Key hyperparameters (starting points)
- RPC tolerance П„=1eвҲ’3 (Case A), 3eвҲ’3 (Case C); max nodes 256.
- Probes m_init=8; align_rate=0.1; Hutch++ sk=2.
- Critics: trunk width 128, 2вҖ“3 layers; head width 32; Adam 1eвҲ’3; 1вҖ“3k steps; Neumann precond depth 2.
- MLMC: base step О”t0=T/4, L up to 5; N_l from pilot runs.

Outputs/analysis
- Plots: error vs T slopes; variance bars per coefficient; runtime vs d; CVR; RPC nodes vs tolerance; m vs alignment on/off; sparsity PR in Case A.
- Success criteria: slopes match K+1 within Вұ0.2; вүҘ5Г— runtime improvement vs denseвҖ‘Jac at d=64; CVRвүҘ10Г— on at least half of nonzero coefficients; correct pruning of вүҘ95% zero brackets with <1% false positives.

Implementation snippets (sketch)
- JVP Stratonovich correction:
  def strato_b(b, sigmas, x):
      corr = 0
      for sj in sigmas:
          v = sj(x)
          jvp = functorch.jvp(sj, (x,), (v,))[1]
          corr = corr + 0.5*jvp
      return b(x) - corr
- Lie bracket via JVPs: [V,W](x)вүҲJ_W(x)V(x) вҲ’ J_V(x)W(x) via jvp on W and V along each other, no dense Jacobians.
- Hutch++ contraction skeleton:
  def hutchpp_trace(op_mul, d, m):
      # op_mul: function R^{dГ—k}->R^{dГ—k}
      G = torch.randn(d, m)
      Y = op_mul(G)
      Q,_ = torch.linalg.qr(Y, mode='reduced')
      B = Q.T @ op_mul(Q)
      Z = torch.randn(d, m)
      return torch.trace(B) + (Z - Q@(Q.T@Z)).T.view(-1) @ op_mul(Z - Q@(Q.T@Z)).view(-1) / m


Experiment 2: HighвҖ‘dimensional Greeks under localвҖ‘vol with lowвҖ‘rank diffusion
Objective and hypotheses
- Demonstrate computational and statistical gains for parameter and initialвҖ‘value sensitivities (Greeks) in d up to 2k with rвүӘd.
- H1: For basket options with stateвҖ‘dependent Пғ, HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ yields lower MSE/time than pathwise and LR estimators at equal compute, and scales with r not d.
- H2: CoefficientвҖ‘specific critics and multiвҖ‘task RPC reduce variance and compute вүҘ5Г— while preserving bias control via MLMC/timeвҖ‘rand.

Model
- d assets S_tвҲҲR^d, dвҲҲ{256, 1k, 2k}; rвҲҲ{3,5}. Dynamics (ItГҙ):
  dS_t = diag(S_t) [ (Ој вҲ’ 0.5 v(S_t;Оё)) dt + L(S_t;Оё) dW_t ],
  where L(S;Оё)=U diag(в„“(S;Оё)) with UвҲҲR^{dГ—r} fixed factors (orthonormal cols), в„“_i(S;Оё)=Пғ0_i (1 + Оұ_i tanh(ОІ_i (log S_i вҲ’ m_i))). v = diag(L LбөҖ).
- Observable: Basket call g(S_T)=max( (1/d)вҲ‘ w_i S_{i,T} вҲ’ K, 0 ).
- Greeks: Delta along direction u (вҲӮ/вҲӮОө E[g(S_T(1+Оөu))] at Оө=0), Vega parameterized by Оё=(Оұ,ОІ) vector; optionally rho wrt Ој.

Compared methods
- HiLoвҖ‘STeP++: Use generatorвҖ‘gradient surrogate to estimate вҲҮ_Оё of Taylor coefficients and reconstruct вҲӮ_Оё E[g] up to K=2 (and optionally K=3); use mollified payoff or conditional smoothing near kink.
- Baselines: Pathwise (with payoff smoothing ОөвҶ’0), Likelihood Ratio (score function), generatorвҖ‘gradient baseline if available, and naГҜve MC finiteвҖ‘difference wrt Оё (for small d only).

Metrics
- Accuracy: Relative error of Greeks vs highвҖ‘precision MLMC reference (О”t small, large N) for dвүӨ256; for higher d report convergence vs compute and crossвҖ‘check on coarser dimensions.
- Variance/time: Variance per unit wallвҖ‘time; MSE at fixed time budget; scaling vs d and r.
- Stability: Gradient variance across seeds; sensitivity to payoff smoothing Оө.

Protocol
1) Build simulator in torchsde with Stratonovich conversion when needed for bracket terms; couple Brownian paths across MLMC levels.
2) RPC over (t,S_t) using moment prior from lognormal approximation: m(t)=E[S_t] via geometric Brownian moments under local vol linearization; P(t) from assumedвҖ‘density evolution.
3) Coefficient selection: For Delta, target coefficients that most influence вҲӮ/вҲӮx E[g], guided by observableвҖ‘aware library; for Vega/Оё use вҲӮ/вҲӮОё of the same coefficients via shared JVP infra.
4) Critics: Shared trunk with perвҖ‘Greek heads; precondition in diffusion subspace; Stein warmвҖ‘start for 500 steps; then train online for 2k steps while collecting samples.
5) MLMC+time randomization: L up to 5; sample allocation from pilot variance; share probe seeds across levels; optional light drift tilting towards large proxy |h_c| regions.
6) Compute Greeks with K=2 expansion and report error/time; repeat with K=1 to show gain from higher order.
7) Ablations: +/вҲ’ critics, +/вҲ’ RPC, +/вҲ’ subspace restriction, +/вҲ’ probe alignment, +/вҲ’ MLMC/timeвҖ‘rand.

Hyperparameters
- T=0.5; K=2 default; О”t0=T/4; Оө_smooth for payoff: 1eвҲ’3 (anneal to 0 for reporting via Richardson extrapolation).
- RPC П„=2eвҲ’3; max nodes 128; m_init=8 probes; alignment on.
- Critics: trunk 256 width, 3 layers; head 64; cosine decay LR 1eвҲ’3вҶ’1eвҲ’4.

Outputs/analysis
- MSE vs time for each Greek and method; scaling plots vs d and r; CVR; node counts; probes used; gradient variance histograms.
- Success criteria: At d=1k, r=5, HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ achieves вүҘ5Г— lower MSE/time than best baseline; stable across seeds; benefit from K=2 over K=1.

Implementation sketch for Greek via coefficient reconstruction
- Compute coefficient estimates C_k and their ОёвҖ‘derivatives dC_k/dОё.
- Approximate E[g]вүҲвҲ‘_{k=0}^K C_k; then вҲӮ_Оё E[g]вүҲвҲ‘_{k=0}^K dC_k/dОё.
- Use the same MLMC/timeвҖ‘rand samples for both to reduce variance.


Experiment 3: Robustness under nonвҖ‘smooth drifts with mollification and Stein deвҖ‘biasing
Objective and hypotheses
- Show that the method remains stable when drift is only piecewise smooth (e.g., ReLU network), where classical estimators degrade.
- H1: Light Gaussian mollification + Stein deвҖ‘biasing controls bias without exploding variance; critics further reduce variance.

Model
- dвҲҲ{128, 512}, r=5. Drift b(x)=W2 ReLU(W1 x) вҲ’ О» x with W1,W2 random Gaussian (frozen); Пғ(x)=U diag(вҲҡ(a + b вҠҷ x^2)); Stratonovich needed for bracket logic.
- Observables: g1(x)=xбөҖQx, g2(x)=tanh(vбөҖx), and a nonвҖ‘smooth surrogate g3(x)=max(uбөҖx,0) smoothed with Оө.

Methods
- HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ with:
  a) no mollification (control);
  b) Gaussian mollification xвҶ’x+О¶, О¶~N(0, s^2 I) inside drift evaluations only;
  c) (b) + Stein deвҖ‘biasing using score under moment prior N(m(t),P(t)) to cancel firstвҖ‘order mollification bias.
- Baselines: Pathwise and LR estimators where applicable; generatorвҖ‘gradient baseline if available.

Metrics
- Bias vs variance vs smoothing scale s; total MSE; robustness across seeds; computational overhead.

Protocol
1) For sвҲҲ{0, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05}, estimate coefficients and reconstruct E[g(X_T)] (K=2) under fixed compute.
2) Apply Stein deвҖ‘biasing: fit score of N(m(t),P(t)) and add firstвҖ‘order correction to remove mollification bias.
3) Train critics with Stein warmвҖ‘start to avoid second derivatives; measure CVR.
4) Ablations: With/without diffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning; with/without drift tilting to improve occupancy.

Hyperparameters
- T=0.3; О”t0=T/8; RPC П„=3eвҲ’3; m_init=10; critics trunk 128.

Outputs/analysis
- Plots: MSE vs s; variance vs s; bias vs s; effect of Stein deвҖ‘biasing; runtime overhead.
- Success criteria: With sвүҲ0.02 and Stein deвҖ‘biasing, MSE within 1.2Г— of smoothвҖ‘drift baseline while noвҖ‘mollification fails (e.g., variance >10Г— or numerical instability), and CVRвүҘ5Г—.


Common implementation details and diagnostics
- Libraries: PyTorch 2.x, functorch (vmap, jvp), torchsde for SDE integration (Stratonovich/ItГҙ), numpy, matplotlib.
- Reproducibility: torch.manual_seed, fixed PRNG for Brownian paths and probe seeds; deterministic cudnn if using CNNs.
- Diagnostics to log per run:
  вҖў Estimated active rank rМӮ and spectrum decay at RPC nodes.
  вҖў Probe count m selected by jackknife and variance estimates.
  вҖў RPC residuals vs node count; node locations in time.
  вҖў Critic losses (Poisson residual, Stein loss), CV gap.
  вҖў MLMC perвҖ‘level variances, costs, and effective sample sizes.
  вҖў Decomposition of MSE terms when possible: discretization, probing, CV misspecification, RPC residual.
- Risk mitigation:
  вҖў Start with K=1, verify baselines, then enable K=2/3.
  вҖў Validate Stratonovich converter on toy 2D examples.
  вҖў For highвҖ‘d, profile memory; use checkpointing and miniвҖ‘batch RPC nodes.

Deliverables per experiment
- Clean scripts: exp1_synthetic.py, exp2_greeks.py, exp3_nonsmooth.py.
- Config files for d,r,T,K, П„, m_init, MLMC levels.
- Saved results (JSON/CSV) with metrics; plotting scripts.
- Ablation toggles via CLI flags.

Expected impact across experiments
- Demonstrate correctness (order slopes), scalability (rвҖ‘driven cost), and strong variance reductions from coefficientвҖ‘aware critics, RPC, and MLMC/timeвҖ‘randomization; show robustness under nonвҖ‘smooth drifts with principled deвҖ‘biasing.
experimental_design.experiment_details:
Title: Experimental plans for validating HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ (KвүӨ3 weakвҖ‘Taylor coefficient estimation via JVPвҖ‘only LieвҖ‘bracket probing, multiвҖ‘task critics, RPC, MLMC)

Overview and integration across experiments
- Goal: Validate correctness, scalability, and robustness of HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ using three complementary settings: (1) synthetic SDEs with analytic/highвҖ‘precision references; (2) highвҖ‘dimensional Greeks with local volatility; (3) nonвҖ‘smooth drifts with mollification and Stein deвҖ‘biasing.
- Design principles for reliability and nonвҖ‘overlap:
  вҖў Shared core implementation (JVP bracket engine, diffusionвҖ‘subspace discovery, Hutch++ probing, RPC node selection with GP, multiвҖ‘task critics, MLMC/time randomization, optional tilting) with configuration toggles for ablations.
  вҖў Each experiment focuses on a distinct stress: Experiment 1 emphasizes correctness and scaling; Experiment 2 emphasizes parameter sensitivities and applicationвҖ‘level MSE/time; Experiment 3 emphasizes robustness to nonвҖ‘smoothness and deвҖ‘biasing.
  вҖў Common diagnostic suite (variance decomposition, controlвҖ‘variate gaps, RPC residuals, probe counts, active rank spectra) ensures consistent comparison across experiments while avoiding duplicated evaluation steps.
- Libraries: PyTorch 2.x; torchsde; BoTorch+GPyTorch (for RPC as GPвҖ‘based residual selection and multiвҖ‘task modeling); functorch or torch.autograd.functional for JVP; NumPy; Matplotlib; optionally torch.linalg for randomized range finding.
- Reproducibility: torch.manual_seed; fixed RNG streams for Brownian paths, probe seeds, and GP inducing point selection; deterministic cuDNN flags when needed.

Global implementation building blocks (used by all experiments)
1) JVPвҖ‘only HallвҖ‘basis LieвҖ‘bracket engine up to K=3
- Stratonovich drift converter b^вҲҳ(x) = b(x) вҲ’ 0.5 ОЈ_j JПғ_j(x) Пғ_j(x) via directional JVPs (no dense Jacobians).
- Bracket via JVP: [V, W](x) = J_W(x)V(x) вҲ’ J_V(x)W(x) computed by jvp on W and V along directions V(x) and W(x), respectively.
- Batched vmap for probes and RPC nodes.

Example code (assuming PyTorch 2.x; fall back to functorch if needed):

import torch
from torch.autograd.functional import jvp

# Vector fields are callables R^d->R^d; they may close over parameters/Оё
def stratonovich_drift(b, sigmas, x):
    corr = torch.zeros_like(x)
    for sj in sigmas:  # sj: x -> R^d
        v = sj(x)
        _, jvp_val = jvp(sj, (x,), (v,), create_graph=False, strict=True)
        corr = corr + 0.5 * jvp_val
    return b(x) - corr

def lie_bracket(V, W, x):
    Vx = V(x)
    Wx = W(x)
    _, JW_Vx = jvp(W, (x,), (Vx,), create_graph=False, strict=True)
    _, JV_Wx = jvp(V, (x,), (Wx,), create_graph=False, strict=True)
    return JW_Vx - JV_Wx

# Example of nested bracket [V, [W, U]]
def nested_bracket(V, W, U, x):
    def inner(z):
        return lie_bracket(W, U, z)
    return lie_bracket(V, lambda z: inner(z), x)

2) DiffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction and probe alignment
- Local active diffusion subspace estimation at node (t, x): randomized range finder on Пғ(x) вҲҲ R^{dГ—r_full} using a few Gaussian/Hadamard probes.
- Keep top r_hat columns spanning range(Пғ); all bracket probing restricted to this subspace.
- Oja updates to align probe directions to dominant curvature of bracket contractions, reducing required probe count m.

Example code (randomized range + single power iteration + Oja update):

def estimate_diffusion_subspace(sigma_columns, x, q=16, power_iter=1):
    # sigma_columns: list of callable sigma_j(x) returning R^d vectors
    d = x.numel()
    r_full = len(sigma_columns)
    # Build matrix action S: R^{r_full} -> R^d by stacking sigma_j(x)
    S = torch.stack([sj(x) for sj in sigma_columns], dim=1)  # d Г— r_full
    # Randomized range finder on S
    Omega = torch.randn(r_full, q, device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
    Y = S @ Omega
    for _ in range(power_iter):
        Y = S @ (S.T @ Y)
    Q, _ = torch.linalg.qr(Y, mode='reduced')  # d Г— q
    # r_hat from spectrum of S^T S projected
    B = Q.T @ S  # q Г— r_full
    U_s, Sv, Vt = torch.linalg.svd(B, full_matrices=False)
    # Choose r_hat by energy threshold
    energy = (Sv**2).cumsum(0) / (Sv**2).sum()
    r_hat = int(torch.searchsorted(energy, torch.tensor(0.99, device=energy.device)).item() + 1)
    Ur = (Q @ U_s[:, :r_hat])  # d Г— r_hat
    return Ur  # columnвҖ‘orthonormal basis for local diffusion subspace

# Oja alignment for probes toward top curvature
@torch.no_grad()
def oja_update(probes, gradop_apply, lr=0.1):
    # probes: d Г— m; gradop_apply: function dГ—m -> dГ—m of symmetric op
    Y = gradop_apply(probes)
    probes = probes + lr * Y
    # orthonormalize
    Q, _ = torch.linalg.qr(probes, mode='reduced')
    return Q

3) Randomized contractions via Hutch++ with subspace restriction
- Given a symmetric linear operator A (induced by a bracket functional or Gram), estimate traces or selected contractions using Hutch++ with orthogonalized probes, restricted to Ur subspace.

Example code:

def hutchpp_trace(op_mul, d, m=8, Q_init=None):
    # op_mul: callable R^{dГ—k} -> R^{dГ—k}
    G = torch.randn(d, m, device=next(op_mul.parameters(), torch.zeros(1)).device) if hasattr(op_mul, 'parameters') else torch.randn(d, m)
    Y = op_mul(G)
    Q, _ = torch.linalg.qr(Y, mode='reduced')
    if Q_init is not None:
        # Blend with aligned probes
        Q = torch.linalg.qr(torch.cat([Q, Q_init], dim=1), mode='reduced')[0]
    B = Q.T @ op_mul(Q)  # small matrix
    # Residual part
    Z = torch.randn(d, m, device=Q.device, dtype=Q.dtype)
    PZ = Z - Q @ (Q.T @ Z)
    R = op_mul(PZ)
    trace_resid = torch.sum(PZ * R) / m
    return torch.trace(B) + trace_resid

4) RPC over timeвҖ“state via multiвҖ‘task GP (BoTorch/GPyTorch)
- We model h_c(t, x) jointly for multiple coefficients c with an ICM multiвҖ‘task GP using a kernel on whitened (t, x) coordinates: k((t, x), (t', x')) = k_t(t, t') Г— k_x( (xвҲ’m(t))/P(t)^{1/2}, (x'вҲ’m(t'))/P(t')^{1/2} ) with automatic relevance determination.
- Node selection: greedy variance reduction. At each step, choose candidate that maximizes sum of posterior variances across tasks; stop when integrated posterior variance (proxy to RPC residual) drops below tolerance П„.
- Candidate pool: draw from occupancy prior N(m(t), P(t)) on a grid of times; optionally reweight by proxy Var[h_c].

Sketch code (ICM GP and greedy selection):

# pip install gpytorch botorch
import torch
import gpytorch
from botorch.models.multitask import MultiTaskGP
from botorch.fit import fit_gpytorch_mll
from botorch.acquisition import PosteriorMean
from botorch.optim import optimize_acqf

# Prepare training data (initial few points) X: NГ—(1+d), Y: NГ—C
# Whitening is applied before fitting

def fit_multitask_gp(X, Y, task_indices):
    # X: NГ—(1+d), task_indices: N (0..C-1), Y: N
    model = MultiTaskGP(X, Y, task_indices=task_indices, rank=1)
    mll = gpytorch.mlls.ExactMarginalLogLikelihood(model.likelihood, model)
    fit_gpytorch_mll(mll)
    return model

# Greedy selection from a candidate pool Xcand (MГ—(1+d))
@torch.no_grad()
def select_rpc_nodes(model, Xcand, K_add, tasks, tau, current_nodes):
    selected = []
    while len(selected) < K_add:
        post = model.posterior(Xcand, observation_noise=False)
        # Sum of marginal variances across tasks
        var = post.variance  # shape: MГ—C
        score = var.sum(dim=-1)
        idx = torch.argmax(score).item()
        selected.append(Xcand[idx])
        # Update training set with pseudoвҖ‘observation at mean to reduce variance
        # (equivalent to DвҖ‘optimal greedy; in code, actually refit or update fantasy model)
        current_nodes = torch.cat([current_nodes, Xcand[idx][None]], dim=0)
        # In practice use fantasy models (botorch.models.model_fantasize)
        if score[idx] < tau:
            break
    return torch.stack(selected, dim=0)

5) CoefficientвҖ‘specific critics (Poisson/BSDE control variates)
- Shared trunk with perвҖ‘coefficient heads; gradientвҖ‘network variant to avoid second derivatives.
- Train with detachвҖ‘style losses approximating L c вүҲ h on collocation nodes (t, x) drawn from RPC. DiffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning: project gradient onto Ur.
- Use Stein warmвҖ‘start under moment prior to stabilise early training.

Sketch code (multiвҖ‘head critic with gradient head):

import torch.nn as nn

class CriticNet(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d, C, hidden=128):
        super().__init__()
        self.trunk = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d+1, hidden), nn.SiLU(),
                                   nn.Linear(hidden, hidden), nn.SiLU())
        self.heads = nn.ModuleList([nn.Linear(hidden, 1) for _ in range(C)])
        # Optional gradient network head (predicts grad wrt x)
        self.grad_heads = nn.ModuleList([nn.Linear(hidden, d) for _ in range(C)])
    def forward(self, t, x):
        # t: NГ—1, x: NГ—d
        h = self.trunk(torch.cat([t, x], dim=-1))
        vals = [head(h) for head in self.heads]
        grads = [ghead(h) for ghead in self.grad_heads]
        return torch.cat(vals, dim=-1), grads

# Loss components
# L c вүҲ h: Lc = bВ·вҲҮc + 0.5 Tr[ПғПғ^T вҲҮ^2 c]; avoid Hessian by gradientвҖ‘network variant + Neumann preconditioner

def poisson_loss(critic, batch, coeff_targets, b, sigma_cols, Ur):
    t, x, h_targets = batch  # shapes NГ—1, NГ—d, NГ—C
    c_vals, grad_preds = critic(t, x)
    # project gradients to diffusion subspace
    Urx = Ur  # dГ—r_hat
    loss = 0.0
    for ci in range(h_targets.shape[-1]):
        grad = grad_preds[ci]  # NГ—d
        grad_proj = grad @ (Urx @ Urx.T)  # NГ—d
        # approximate Lc using projected gradients; 2ndвҖ‘order handled by Neumann or ignored initially
        drift_term = torch.sum(b(x) * grad_proj, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        # simple surrogate for diffusion term via directional JVPs if needed (omitted for brevity)
        res = drift_term - h_targets[:, ci:ci+1]
        loss = loss + (res**2).mean()
    return loss

# Stein warmвҖ‘start: minimize E[(вҲҮВ·(P^{-1}(xвҲ’m) c) + (P^{-1}(xвҲ’m))В·вҲҮc)] under Gaussian prior; implement with samples from prior

6) MLMC with time randomization and sharedвҖ‘seed coupling
- Convert path integrals вҲ«_0^T h(t, X_t) dt to T В· E[h(П„, X_П„)] with П„ ~ Uniform[0, T].
- Use torchsde BrownianInterval to couple fine and coarse levels; share Brownian and probe seeds across levels.
- Adaptive allocation: choose N_l вҲқ sqrt(V_l / cost_l) with pilot estimates; stop when variance and bias tolerances met.

Sketch code (level simulator and allocation):

import torchsde

class SDE(torchsde.SDEIto):
    def __init__(self, b, sigma_cols):
        super().__init__(noise_type='general', sde_type='ito')
        self.b = b; self.sigma_cols = sigma_cols
    def f(self, t, y):
        return self.b(y)
    def g(self, t, y):
        # stack columns into dГ—r
        return torch.stack([sj(y) for sj in self.sigma_cols], dim=-1)

@torch.no_grad()
def simulate_level(sde, x0, T, dt, seed):
    w = torchsde.BrownianInterval(0.0, T, shape=x0.shape, device=x0.device, dtype=x0.dtype, seed=seed)
    ys = torchsde.sdeint(sde, x0, torch.tensor([0.0, T], device=x0.device), dt=dt, bm=w, adaptive=False)
    return ys[-1]

# MLMC allocation after pilot

def allocate_mlmc(costs, variances, N_total):
    weights = torch.sqrt(variances / costs)
    nl = (N_total * weights / weights.sum()).clamp_min(10).to(torch.int64)
    return nl

7) Girsanov drift tilting (optional)
- Tilt drift by small Оҙb toward high |h_c| regions; correct with RadonвҖ“Nikodym weight: 
  w = exp(вҲ«_0^T Пғ^вҖ  Оҙb В· dW вҲ’ 0.5 вҲ«_0^T ||Пғ^вҖ  Оҙb||^2 dt). Implement with discretized sum along simulated path if used.

Sketch weight computation:

def girsanov_weight(sigmat, delta_b_path, dW_path, dt):
    # sigmat: function giving Пғ at path states; delta_b_path: NstepsГ—d; dW_path: NstepsГ—r; dt scalar
    # Пғ^вҖ  Оҙb via least squares in diffusion subspace
    w_inc = []
    for k in range(delta_b_path.shape[0]):
        sigma_k = sigmat(k)  # dГ—r
        pinv = torch.linalg.pinv(sigma_k)
        z = pinv.T @ delta_b_path[k]  # r
        w_inc.append((z * dW_path[k]).sum() - 0.5 * (z @ z) * dt)
    return torch.exp(torch.stack(w_inc).sum())

8) ProbeвҖ‘count controller via jackknife
- Split m probes, compute leaveвҖ‘oneвҖ‘out estimates, estimate probeвҖ‘induced variance; increase m until relative SE below threshold.

Sketch:

def adapt_probes(estimates_per_probe, rel_tol=0.1, m_max=64):
    m = estimates_per_probe.shape[0]
    mean = estimates_per_probe.mean(0)
    diffs = estimates_per_probe - mean
    se = diffs.pow(2).mean(0).sqrt() / m**0.5
    if (se / (mean.abs() + 1e-8)).max() > rel_tol and m < m_max:
        return int(min(m_max, max(m+4, m*3//2)))
    return m

9) Metrics, logging, diagnostics
- Track: accuracy vs references, variance and CVR for each coefficient; runtime breakdown (JVPs, GP/RPC, critic train, SDE sim); RPC residual vs nodes; active rank spectra; perвҖ‘level MLMC variances; probe counts; CV gaps; sparsity PR.
- Use bootstrap 95% CIs over seeds; for sparsity screening, BHвҖ‘corrected pвҖ‘values with Hutchinson null.

Experiment 1: Synthetic linear/nonlinear SDEs with groundвҖ‘truth or highвҖ‘precision references
Objective and hypotheses
- Validate correctness and variance gains of coefficient targeting, JVPвҖ‘only bracket probing, diffusionвҖ‘subspace restriction, probe alignment, and coefficientвҖ‘specific critics.
- H1: K=2/3 coefficients reconstruct E[g(X_T)] with O(T^{K+1}) residual and nearвҖ‘zero bias as О”tвҶ’0.
- H2: Runtime scales with effective rank r and probes mвүҲO(log r), not O(d^2).
- H3: Critics reduce variance вүҘ10Г— on nontrivial coefficients; RPC cuts node count вүҘ3Г— at fixed tolerance.

Models and observables
- Case A (commuting diffusions, sparsity check):
  вҖў d вҲҲ {256, 2000, 10000}, r=8. Пғ_j(x) = e_j f_j(x_j) with smooth bounded f_j; b(x)=вҲ’Оӣx + c, Оӣ diagonal.
  вҖў Observable g(x)=x^T Q x with sparse banded Q.
  вҖў Many brackets vanish: [Пғ_i, Пғ_j]=0 for iвү j, [Пғ_i, [Пғ_j, Пғ_k]]=0.
- Case B (nonвҖ‘commuting, lowвҖ‘rank linear, analytic reference for small d):
  вҖў d вҲҲ {16, 64}, r вҲҲ {2, 4}. Stratonovich linear fields b(x)=A x, Пғ_k(x)=B_k x.
  вҖў Observable g(x)=x^T Q x. Analytic E[xx^T] via Lyapunov ODE; smallвҖ‘T bracket coefficients via commutators [B_i, B_j], [A, B_i].
- Case C (nonlinear, highвҖ‘d scaling):
  вҖў d вҲҲ {512, 2000}, r вҲҲ {4, 8}. b(x)=вҲ’Оӣx + О· tanh(Cx), Пғ(x)=U diag(s(x)), s_i(x)=sqrt(a_i + b_i x_i^2), U orthonormal dГ—r.
  вҖў Observables: g2(x)=x^T Q x; g3(x)=вҲ‘ w_i x_i^3 (exercise K=3).

Ground truth/reference
- Case A: Zero coefficients identified analytically; nonzeros via highвҖ‘precision MC (NвүҲ1e7, О”t=2^вҲ’12) or MLMC reference.
- Case B: Exact E[g(X_T)] via matrix ODE; for small T, compute orderвҖ‘2 bracket coefficients analytically and compare termвҖ‘wise.
- Case C: HighвҖ‘precision MLMC (L=6, tuned N_l) for E[g(X_T)] to validate reconstructed expectations.

Compared methods
- HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ full: JVP DLP + diffusion subspace + Oja alignment + Hutch++ + multiвҖ‘task RPC + coefficientвҖ‘specific critics + time randomization + MLMC coupling.
- Ablations: remove one component at a time (no RPC; no critics; no subspace restriction; no alignment; Hutchinson only; no MLMC/timeвҖ‘rand; singleвҖ‘task critics; no bracketвҖ‘sparsity screening; naive Gaussian probes).
- DenseвҖ‘Jac baseline (dвүӨ64): explicitly form Jacobians/Hessians for brackets and coefficients.

Key procedures and settings
- BracketвҖ‘sparsity screening (Case A): For each candidate node (t, x) from occupancy prior, run 4вҖ“8 Hutchinson probes on each basis element; compute twoвҖ‘sided tвҖ‘test of mean contraction vs 0; BH FDR 5%; prune failing brackets before full evaluation.
- Diffusion subspace: randomized range finder with q=16 probes, one power iteration; retain 99% energy. Record r_hat and spectrum.
- Probes: start m=8; adapt by jackknife to reach relative SE < 10% or m_max=64.
- RPC: П„=1eвҲ’3 (Case A), 3eвҲ’3 (Case C); candidates per time grid: 1024; select вүӨ256 nodes. Use multiвҖ‘task GP with ICM.
- Critics: trunk width 128, 2вҖ“3 layers; head width 32; Adam 1eвҲ’3; Stein warmвҖ‘start 250 steps; then 1вҖ“3k detachвҖ‘style steps; Neumann depth 2; diffusionвҖ‘subspace preconditioning.
- MLMC: base step О”t0=T/4, LвүӨ5; time randomization on; allocate via pilot.

Evaluation metrics and analysis
- Accuracy: absolute/relative error of each coefficient and reconstructed E[g(X_T)] using K=2/3; logвҖ“log slope vs T in {0.05, 0.1, 0.2}; expect slope K+1 within Вұ0.2.
- Variance: perвҖ‘coefficient sample variance; CVR = Var(noвҖ‘CV)/Var(withвҖ‘CV); node counts vs П„; probes vs alignment on/off.
- Efficiency: wallвҖ‘time to ОөвҖ‘MSE; scaling vs d and r; number of JVPs; memory; precision/recall of sparsity detection (Case A) with FDR control.

Experiment 2: HighвҖ‘dimensional Greeks under localвҖ‘vol with lowвҖ‘rank diffusion
Objective and hypotheses
- Show computational/statistical gains for parameter and initialвҖ‘value sensitivities up to d=2000 with rвүӘd.
- H1: Lower MSE/time than pathwise and LR estimators; scaling with r not d.
- H2: Critics and multiвҖ‘task RPC reduce variance/compute вүҘ5Г— with MLMC/timeвҖ‘rand preserving bias.

Model and observables
- Assets S_t вҲҲ R^d with ItГҙ dynamics: dS_t = diag(S_t)[(Ој вҲ’ 0.5 v(S_t; Оё))dt + L(S_t; Оё) dW_t], L(S; Оё)=U diag(в„“(S; Оё)), U dГ—r orthonormal.
- в„“_i(S; Оё)=Пғ0_i (1 + Оұ_i tanh(ОІ_i (log S_i вҲ’ m_i))). v = diag(LL^T).
- Payoff: basket call g(S_T)=max((1/d)вҲ‘ w_i S_i вҲ’ K, 0). Smooth with Оө for pathwise differentiation; report RichardsonвҖ‘extrapolated ОөвҶ’0.
- Greeks: Delta along a given direction u; Vega wrt Оё=(Оұ, ОІ); optionally rho (Ој sensitivity).

HiLoвҖ‘STeP++ setup for Greeks
- ObservableвҖ‘aware mapping: choose Taylor coefficients impacting вҲӮ_x E[g] and вҲӮ_Оё E[g]; compute C_k and dC_k/dОё via shared JVP infrastructure.
- Implementation: Use the same path samples and probe seeds to coвҖ‘estimate C_k and their ОёвҖ‘derivatives to reduce variance of the Greek estimator sums.
- RPC: prior moments via lognormal approximation: m(t)=E[S_t], P(t) from assumedвҖ‘density evolution under linearized local vol; whiten (t, S) for kernel; multiвҖ‘task over GreekвҖ‘specific coefficients.
- Critics: shared trunk with perвҖ‘Greek heads; diffusion subspace preconditioning; Stein warmвҖ‘start 500 steps; online 2k steps.
- MLMC/time randomization: LвүӨ5; adaptive allocation; coupled Brownian and probe seeds across levels.
- Drift tilting (optional): small tilt toward large |h_c| regions identified by proxy; monitor ESS via weight variance; disable if ESS falls below threshold.

Baselines
- Pathwise with payoff smoothing; LR (scoreвҖ‘function) estimator; generatorвҖ‘gradient baseline (if available) with dense flows for small d; finite differences for Оё in small d.

Hyperparameters
- T=0.5; K=2 (with K=1 as ablation); О”t0=T/4; Оө_smooth=1eвҲ’3 with extrapolation.
- RPC П„=2eвҲ’3; max nodes 128; m_init=8, Oja alignment on.
- Critics trunk 256Г—3; heads 64; Adam with cosine LR 1eвҲ’3вҶ’1eвҲ’4.

Metrics and success criteria
- MSE vs time for each Greek and method; scaling vs d вҲҲ {256, 1000, 2000} and r вҲҲ {3, 5}.
- CVR per Greek; node/probe counts; stability across seeds.
- Success: at d=1000, r=5, вүҘ5Г— lower MSE/time than best baseline; clear advantage of K=2 over K=1.

def greek_from_coeffs(C_list, dC_dtheta_list):
